message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="blue" summary="Design confirmed, native radios, short legends, show-all fallback, no explanatory note">
Steve, that updated clarify design is spot-on. The visual structure is clear, the radio groups with fieldset legends are correct, and the left indent for conditional sections is a nice visual affordance that communicates dependency without relying on ARIA.

A few notes on your design, then the answer to your fallback question:

## Native Radio Buttons -- Exactly Right

Your instinct to use native radios with CSS enhancement (not custom-styled replacements) is correct. Custom radio buttons are one of the most common sources of a11y bugs -- developers replace the native element with a styled `<div>` and forget to wire up keyboard behavior, focus indicators, and ARIA states. Native `<input type="radio">` gives you all of that for free. You can style the label and the surrounding area as much as you want, and use CSS `accent-color` to tint the native radio to match your design tokens.

## Fieldset/Legend -- One Nuance

Using `<fieldset>` with `<legend>` for each radio group is the correct semantic pattern. One nuance: screen readers announce the legend text before every radio button in the group. So if the legend is "Is this actionable?" and the options are "Yes" and "No," a screen reader user hears:

- "Is this actionable? Yes, radio button, 1 of 2"
- "Is this actionable? No, radio button, 2 of 2"

This repetition is expected and helpful -- it provides context. But keep legend text short. "Is this actionable?" is good. "Please select whether this inbox item represents something you need to take action on" is too long and will be announced before every option.

## "Skip" as a Link -- Correct

"Skip" as a secondary text link (not a button) is right. Skipping doesn't modify data -- it navigates to the next item. Links are for navigation, buttons are for actions. The link should go to `/inbox/{next-id}/clarify` or back to `/inbox` depending on the product decision. I'd suggest it goes to the next item (consistent with the flow-through pattern Carson designed).

## Your Fallback Question: Show Everything, No Note

For the no-JS / no-`:has()` fallback: **show all fields visible, no explanatory note.**

Here's why:
- The form still works. The user sees all the fields, selects the relevant radio buttons, fills in the relevant text inputs, and submits. The server ignores fields that don't apply to the selected option (server-side validation handles this).
- An explanatory note like "Select an option above to see relevant fields" is confusing when all fields ARE visible. It describes behavior that isn't happening.
- Users who don't have `:has()` support are on older browsers. They're accustomed to seeing longer forms. It's "noisier" as you said, but it's functional. That's what progressive enhancement means -- the baseline works, the enhancement makes it nicer.
- The fieldset legends and radio group labels already communicate which fields go with which choice. The form structure itself is the instruction.

The one thing the server MUST handle: if the user selects "No" (not actionable) but also fills in the "Next action title" field (because it was visible in the fallback), the server ignores the next action fields entirely. The radio button selection is the authoritative signal. This is standard form handling -- the server already needs to parse based on the selected option, not blindly accept all fields.

## Overall

Your updated design is clean, accessible, and aligned with the team's consensus. The single-form structure with radio groups, fieldset/legend, and CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure is going to be one of the best-designed clarify flows in any GTD app. And it'll work without JavaScript. That's not something most commercial GTD apps can say.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Heydon's response answers my last open question and provides valuable implementation details. Let me confirm and close out this thread.

input.content:
Heydon, perfect. All four points adopted:

1. **Native radios with `accent-color`** -- style the token system's primary blue onto native radios without replacing the element. Free keyboard behavior, free focus indicators, free ARIA states.

2. **Short legends** -- "Is this actionable?", "What type?", not verbose instructions. Repetition before each radio option is expected and helpful.

3. **Skip as a link to next item** -- navigation, not action. Goes to `/inbox/{next-id}/clarify`.

4. **Fallback: show everything, no note** -- the form works with all fields visible. Server-side validation uses the radio selection as the authoritative signal and ignores irrelevant fields. The fieldset structure is self-documenting.

No remaining open questions from me on the design or a11y front. Every piece of feedback you've given this session has improved the design -- the sidebar pivot, the nav count pattern, weight-over-color hierarchy, and the clarify form correction. Appreciate the rigor.
input.summary: All four points adopted, no remaining questions

routing.summary: All four points adopted, no remaining questions

4. **Fallback: show everything, no note** -- the form works with all fields visible. Server-side validation uses the radio selection as the authoritative signal and ignores irrelevant fields. The fieldset structure is self-documenting.

content.text:
All design conversations are now closed. Here's the final state of every design decision made this session: **Navigation:** - Wrapping header nav (no sidebar) - Single `
` landmark, `aria-current="page"` for active state - Transparent 3px bottom border on all items, color changes for active - Tier 1 links `--font-semibold`, Tier 2 `--font-normal` (weight hierarchy, not color) - Count badges as plain text: `Inbox (3)` - Quick capture input in header, "+" icon on mobile **Clarify Flow:** - Single form with radio buttons + CSS `:has()` progressive disclosure - Native radios with `accent-color`, fieldset/legend for groups - Short legends, left indent for conditional sections - No-JS fallback: all fields visible, server ignores irrelevant ones - "Skip" as a link to next item, not a button - HTMX only on form submit (loads next inbox item) **Lists & Components:** - `.gtd-item` base component with BEM view modifiers - Completed actions removed from Next Actions, visible in project view + collapsed `
` section - Single teal accent color for context tags - Stalled projects: danger text + warning border + inline "Add next action" link - Projects show text count, no progress bar - Inbox badge: calm, muted gray, no red/urgency - Dashboard: 4 stat cards, 2x2 mobile, 4-across desktop **Tokens:** - Tag colors (teal), icon sizes, badge dimensions -- needed - Sidebar tokens, z-index scale -- not needed - Content max-width: 44rem for GTD pages
